revision.comment: Created by translating the page "[[:en:Special:Redirect/revision/1150199013|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]]"
revision.text: {{Infobox religious building|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=|image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG|image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[Category:Infobox religious building with unknown affiliation|*]] [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country. == History == {{Infobox religious building|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} [[Category:Infobox religious building with unknown affiliation|Shinto]] Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan}}{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide}} == Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}} It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=21 June 2021|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}} == Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide}}pagebao (28 March 2019). [https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/ "Taichung Confucius Temple"]. ''Taichung Guide''. Retrieved 21 June 2021. == See also == * National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Articles containing simplified Chinese-language text]] [[Category:Articles containing traditional Chinese-language text]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]]

{{Infobox religious building|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=|image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG|image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} [[Category:Infobox religious building with unknown affiliation|Shinto]] Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=21 June 2021|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]]

It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} [[Category:Infobox religious building with unknown affiliation|Shinto]] Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=21 June 2021|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]]

revision.comment: Reformat 1 citation per [[:Category:CS1 errors: archive-url]]. [[User:GreenC/WaybackMedic_2.5|Wayback Medic 2.5]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Martyrs' shrine in North, Taichung, Taiwan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=June 2021}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Taichung Martyrs' Shrine | native_name = 臺中市忠烈祠 | native_name_lang = zh | image = Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG | image_upright = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Taichung | map_size = 225px | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | coordinates = {{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}} | coordinates_footnotes = | religious_affiliation = | locale = | location = [[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]] | deity = | rite = | sect = | tradition = | festival = <!-- or | festivals = --> | cercle = | sector = | administration = | consecration_year = | organisational_status = <!-- or | organizational_status = --> | functional_status = | heritage_designation = | ownership = | governing_body = | leadership = | bhattaraka = | patron = | religious_features_label = | religious_features = | architect = | architecture_type = martyrs' shrine | architecture_style = | founded_by = | creator = | funded_by = | general_contractor = | established = | groundbreaking = | year_completed = | construction_cost = | date_demolished = <!-- or | date_destroyed = --> | facade_direction = | capacity = | length = | width = | width_nave = | interior_area = | height_max = | dome_quantity = | dome_height_outer = | dome_height_inner = | dome_dia_outer = | dome_dia_inner = | minaret_quantity = | minaret_height = | spire_quantity = | spire_height = | site_area = | temple_quantity = | monument_quantity = | shrine_quantity = | inscriptions = | materials = | elevation_m = <!-- or | elevation_ft = --> | elevation_footnotes = | nrhp = | designated = | added = | refnum = | footnotes = | website = }} [[File:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]]
The '''Taichung Martyr's Shrine''' ({{zh|t=臺中市忠烈祠|s=台中市忠烈祠|first=t|p=Táizhōng Shì Zhōngliècí}}) is a [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|martyrs' shrine]] in [[North District, Taichung|North District]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]] dedicated to heroes of the [[Republic of China]].
==History== {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Taichu Jinja | religious_affiliation = Shinto | image = 臺中神社_(第一代).jpg | deity = [[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]] | consecration_year = 1911 }} The shrine was originally a Shinto shrine to honor [[Taiwan under Japanese rule|Japanese]] heroes. After the [[Retrocession Day|handover of Taiwan from Japan]] to the Republic of China in 1945, the Shinto shrine was reestablished and converted to a shrine that honor the [[National Revolutionary Army]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|access-date=21 June 2021}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{cite web|author=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|website=Taichung Guide|access-date=21 June 2021}}</ref>
==Architecture== The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{cite web|date=15 January 2020|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|title=Martyr Shrine|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau|access-date=21 June 2021}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{cite web|date=14 January 2010|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw|access-date=13 May 2014|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014}}</ref>
==Transportation== The shrine is accessible by bus north of [[Taichung railway station|Taichung Station]] of [[Taiwan Railways Administration|Taiwan Railways]].<ref name="Taichung"/>
==See also== * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]]
==References== {{reflist}}
{{commons category}}
[[Category:1970 establishments in Taiwan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Taichung]] [[Category:Martyrs' shrines in Taiwan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in 1970]]
{{Taiwan-struct-stub}}

== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=21 June 2021|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>

This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=|image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG|image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}}
[[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]]

It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=|image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG|image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=|image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG|image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=|image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG|image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>

It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=|image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG|image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=|image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG|image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}}

It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=|image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG|image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}}

When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=|image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG|image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}}
[[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]]

== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>

== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}}
[[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:redirect]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}}
[[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]]
