{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>

It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>

== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>

It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[North District, Taichung|North]], [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=<!-- or | festivals = -->|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=<!-- or | organizational_status = -->|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=<!-- or | date_destroyed = -->|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=<!-- or | elevation_ft = -->|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}} |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.

When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>

When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>

It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}} |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}} |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>

== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[:en:Chinese architecture|Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>

It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[:en:Chinese architecture|Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[:en:North District, Taichung|North]], [[:en:Taichung|Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[:en:Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[:en:Taichung|Taichung]], [[Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[:en:Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[:en:Chinese architecture|Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}} |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
[[Category:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]]
}}

This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[:en:Chinese architecture|Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}} |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[:en:Chinese architecture|Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[simple:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[zh:臺中市忠烈祠]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[:en:North District, Taichung|North]], [[:en:Taichung|Taichung]], [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[:en:Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[:en:Taichung|Taichung]], [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[:en:Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[:en:Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[:en:Chinese architecture|Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}} |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

[[Category:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]]
}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[simple:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[zh:臺中市忠烈祠]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[:en:North District, Taichung|North]], [[:en:Taichung|Taichung]], [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[:en:Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[:en:Taichung|Taichung]], [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[:en:Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[:en:Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[:en:Chinese architecture|Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}} |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

[[Category:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]]
}}

When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[simple:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]][[zh:臺中市忠烈祠]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichung Martyrs' Shrine|native_name=臺中市忠烈祠|native_name_lang=zh|religious_affiliation=| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taichung ROC Martyr's Shrine.JPG| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_upright=|alt=|caption=|map_type=Taichung|coordinates={{coord|24|09|15.8|N|120|41|28.5|E|type:landmark_region:TW|display=inline,title}}|map_size=225px|map_alt=|map_relief=|map_caption=|location=[[:en:North District, Taichung|North]], [[:en:Taichung|Taichung]], [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]]|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' shrine|architecture_style=|founded_by=|creator=|funded_by=|general_contractor=|established=|groundbreaking=|year_completed=|construction_cost=|date_demolished=|facade_direction=|capacity=|length=|width=|width_nave=|interior_area=|height_max=|dome_quantity=|dome_height_outer=|dome_height_inner=|dome_dia_outer=|dome_dia_inner=|minaret_quantity=|minaret_height=|spire_quantity=|spire_height=|site_area=|temple_quantity=|monument_quantity=|shrine_quantity=|materials=|elevation_m=|inscriptions=|elevation_footnotes=|nrhp=|designated=|added=|refnum=|footnotes=}} [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[:en:Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[:en:Taichung|Taichung]], [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[:en:Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[:en:Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[:en:Chinese architecture|Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}} |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

[[Category:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]]
}}
[[en:Taichung Martyrs' Shrine]]

[[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[:en:Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[:en:Taichung|Taichung]], [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[:en:Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[:en:Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[:en:Chinese architecture|Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}} |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

[[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[:en:Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, [[:en:Taichung|Taichung]], [[:en:Taiwan|Taiwan]]. It's dedicated to the heroes of the [[:en:Taiwan|Republic of China]] who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] [[:en:Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa]] [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional [[:en:Chinese architecture|Chinese architecture]] style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}} |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

{{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan}}|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[:en:Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, {{ill|Taichung|en|Taichung|WD=Q245023|lt=Taichung}}, {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan}}. It's dedicated to the heroes of the {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Republic of China}} who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] {{ill|Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|en|Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|WD=Q3197569|lt=Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa}} [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional {{ill|Chinese architecture|en|Chinese architecture|WD=Q957904|lt=Chinese architecture}} style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}} |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

District, Taichung|en|North District, Taichung|WD=Q569469|lt=North|1=North District, Taichung}}, {{ill|Taichung|en|Taichung|WD=Q245023|lt=Taichung|1=Taichung}}, {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan|1=Taiwan}}|locale=|deity=|rite=|tradition=|sect=|festival=|cercle=|sector=|administration=|consecration_year=|organisational_status=|functional_status=|heritage_designation=|leadership=|ownership=|governing_body=|bhattaraka=|patron=|website=|coordinates_footnotes=|architect=|architecture_type=martyrs' [[File:Taichung_Martyrs'_Shrine.JPG|thumb|Taichung Martyrs' Shrine gate]] The Taichung Martyr's Shrine is a [[:en:Martyrs Shrine|martyrs' shrine]] in North District, {{ill|Taichung|en|Taichung|WD=Q245023|lt=Taichung|1=Taichung}}, {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Taiwan|1=Taiwan}}. It's dedicated to the heroes of the {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|WD=Q865|lt=Republic of China|1=Taiwan}} who died for their country.
== History == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Taichu Jinja|religious_affiliation=Shinto| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=臺中神社_(第一代).jpg|deity=[[Ōkuninushi]] {{ill|Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|en|Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|WD=Q3197569|lt=Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa|1=Prince Kitashirakawa Yoshihisa}} [[Sukunabikona]]|consecration_year=1911}} Originally, the shrine was a Shinto shrine to pay tribute to Japanese heroes. When Taiwan was handed over from Japan to the Republic of China in 1945, the shrine was re-established and transformed into a shrine to honor the National Revolutionary Army. This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional {{ill|Chinese architecture|en|Chinese architecture|WD=Q957904|lt=Chinese architecture|1=Chinese architecture}} style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}} |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

This change happened in 1970..<ref>{{Cite web|title=Martyrs' Shrine in Taiwan|url=http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taipei Municipal Dazhi Junior High School, Taipei, Taiwan|archive-date=15 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140515144610/http://library.taiwanschoolnet.org/gsh2006/gsh4337/index2/index2.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Taichung">{{Cite web|last=pagebao|date=28 March 2019|title=Taichung Confucius Temple|url=https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Guide|archive-date=24 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624002533/https://www.taichung.guide/taichung-confucius-temple/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
== Architecture == The shrine was constructed with traditional {{ill|Chinese architecture|en|Chinese architecture|WD=Q957904|lt=Chinese architecture|1=Chinese architecture}} style.<ref>{{Cite web|date=15 January 2020|title=Martyr Shrine|url=https://travel.taichung.gov.tw/en-us/Attractions/Intro/895/Martyr-Shrine|access-date=21 June 2021|website=Taichung Tourism and Travel Bureau}}</ref> It has a rectangular shape surrounded by courtyards and garden.<ref>{{Cite web|date=14 January 2010|title=Confucius Temple and Martyr's Shrine|url=http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140514002449/http://eng.taichung.gov.tw/siteOld/english.taichung.gov.tw/internet/english/docDetail439d.html?uid=4925&docid=20836|archive-date=14 May 2014|access-date=13 May 2014|publisher=Eng.taichung.gov.tw}}</ref>
== Transportation == The shrine is accessible by bus north of Taichung Station of Taiwan Railways.<ref name="Taichung"/>
== References == {{Reflist}} |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] with unknown affiliation|Shinto]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}
